seo.title: W. Virginia Dental Assistant License (2026) | Fees & Guide
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in West Virginia — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license west virginia, dental assistant certification west virginia, RDA west virginia, DANB CDA west virginia, dental assistant radiography west virginia

board.name: West Virginia Board of Dental Examiners

license_types.title: Registered Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in West Virginia.

steps.title: Create an Account
steps.description: Visit the WV Board of Dental Examiners Online Services Portal and create a new account by clicking 'Register Now' or 'Create an Account'. Complete the registration process, including email verification, to gain access to the application system.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the WV Board of Dental Examiners Online Services Portal and select the application for 'Dental Assistant Registration'. Fill out all required sections of the application completely and accurately.

steps.title: Upload Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary documents through the WV Board of Dental Examiners Online Services Portal. This includes proof of being at least 18 years of age, a high school diploma or equivalent, completion of an accredited dental assisting program OR proof of at least 3 months of continuous employment as a dental assistant, and current CPR certification.

steps.title: Attest to Knowledge of Rules
steps.description: Attest within the WV Board of Dental Examiners Online Services Portal that you have read and understood the Board's rules and statutes relevant to the practice of dental assisting in West Virginia.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required initial registration fee of $30.00 through the WV Board of Dental Examiners Online Services Portal using a credit/debit card or other accepted payment method.

steps.title: Await Board Review
steps.description: After submitting your complete application and all required documents and fees via the Online Services Portal, await review and approval from the West Virginia Board of Dental Examiners. You will be notified of your registration status upon completion of the review process.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the West Virginia Board of Dental Examiners by registration.

faqs.question: Does West Virginia require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, West Virginia requires Registered Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in West Virginia?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in West Virginia.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in West Virginia?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in West Virginia?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $50. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the West Virginia Board of Dental Examiners by registration.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in West Virginia
hero.dek: To apply for a Dental Assistant registration in West Virginia, applicants must submit their application through the WV Board of Dental Examiners Online Services Portal.

official_links.portal_name: WV Board of Dental Examiners Online Services Portal
official_links.registration_steps: Visit the WV Board of Dental Examiners Online Services Portal and click 'Register Now' or 'Create an Account'. Follow the prompts to set up a new user ID and password, then verify your email address to activate your account before logging in to access application services.
